item.title: US extends Russian oil waiver in attempt to lower fuel prices
item.description: Thirty-day licence comes as American voters become frustrated at high cost of petrol and diesel

item.title: US drops criminal fraud charges against billionaire Gautam Adani
item.description: Conglomerate owned by Asia’s richest person had earlier agreed to pay $275mn to settle separate US sanctions violation probe

item.title: Musk’s case against OpenAI dismissed after just two hours of jury deliberations
item.description: Decision hands a legal victory to Sam Altman in a case that had overshadowed the AI lab’s plans to go public

item.title: Dior boss Delphine Arnault says brand will be ‘cautious’ on pricing
item.description: Scion of billionaire Arnault family who own LVMH is attempting a reboot alongside designer Jonathan Anderson

item.title: Uber leaves door open to Delivery Hero takeover by increasing stake
item.description: US group becomes largest shareholder in German company amid consolidation across food delivery sector

item.title: UK prepares multibillion-pound boost for joint stealth jet project
item.description: The estimated £6bn funding would allow Britain, Italy and Japan to agree a long-term contract for the programme

item.title: NextEra bets there’s no such thing as having too much power
item.description: There are few obvious areas of overlap between Dominion Energy and its Florida rival

item.title: Citi strikes €15bn partnership with BlackRock for private European lending
item.description: Deal aims to give bank added firepower to finance private equity dealmaking in Europe and Middle East

item.title: UK ministers considered abandoning HS2 amid spiralling costs
item.description: Analysis earlier this year concluded that decommissioning the scheme could cost as much as continuing to build the railway

item.title: Saint Laurent’s chief says luxury must work harder to retain customers
item.description: Cédric Charbit is part of new team of executives at parent company Kering as it seeks to revive growth

item.title: Tear gas and fist fights overshadow Swatch and Audemars Piguet’s ‘Royal Pop’ launch
item.description: Debut of much-anticipated watch collaboration descends into chaos as customers scuffle in cities from Paris to New York

item.title: Baseball is learning to live with shorter attention spans
item.description: New rules have made games faster and more exciting

item.title: UK plans to relax key banking rules set up after 2008 financial crisis
item.description: Chief secretary to the Treasury says softening ringfencing regulations would free up £80bn for extra lending

item.title: NextEra strikes megadeal with Dominion to create $400bn US utility
item.description: Tie-up would create power behemoth at a time of booming demand for electricity for AI data centres

item.title: Commerzbank rejects €39bn UniCredit takeover offer
item.description: Proposal draws pushback from German lender and Chancellor Friedrich Merz

item.title: Tank maker KNDS comes late to the European defence boom
item.description: The Franco-German group might fare better reviving a previously rejected alliance with Rheinmetall

item.title: The UK must embrace its libraries in the age of AI
item.description: As stewards of vast quantities of data, the sector could play a critical role in fuelling the digital economy

item.title: Ford plans successor to best-selling Fiesta in bid to revive European sales
item.description: US carmaker to launch five new vehicles in the region as part of efforts to reclaim sales lost to Chinese rivals

item.title: Anglo American strikes $3.9bn deal to sell Australia coal business
item.description: Sale to privately owned Dhilmar will help advance UK-listed group’s restructuring programme

item.title: Activist hedge fund pushes Premier Inn owner to put itself up for sale
item.description: Corvex Management says Whitbread has no credible plan to reverse share price slide in past year

item.title: Ryanair suspends guidance after surge in jet fuel price
item.description: Carrier confirms new contract negotiations with chief Michael O’Leary after annual profits rise by a third

item.title: Fears of Samsung strike ease after court ruling
item.description: Shares rise after injunction restricts unions’ ability to halt production at South Korean chipmaker

item.title: China is not Japan
item.description: But its real estate market has been doing a darn good impression

item.title: Private capital firms circle L&G
item.description: Plus, SpaceX’s rocketing value, Japan’s AI, and Zurbarán at The National Gallery

item.title: FirstFT: Mortgage costs jump on Iran war
item.description: Also in today’s newsletter: Labour’s Brexit debate and German unemployment

item.title: Anthropic to brief global financial watchdog on cyber flaws exposed by Mythos
item.description: US tech group will discuss capabilities of its new AI model with members of the Financial Stability Board

item.title: European oil refiners and airlines confident jet fuel shortages can be avoided
item.description: Refiners maximise production and increase imports from US and Africa to offset loss of Middle Eastern supplies

item.title: EU plans to force companies to buy parts from non-Chinese suppliers
item.description: New rules to target chemicals and industrial machinery makers

item.title: Defence groups clamour to delay US ban on Chinese rare earth magnets
item.description: Military contractors want more time to comply with prohibition years in the making and now just months away

item.title: Jim Ratcliffe’s Ineos takes €200mn punt on chemical sector peers
item.description: Group invests in basket of company equities and tells bondholders the sector is ‘undervalued’
